compiled_state.title: Retro 2026-04-16: Error resilience — systematic fail-open coverage
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Context
DSO plugin hooks use 3 inconsistent error handling patterns: silent fail-open (5 post-* dispatchers), logging fail-open with copy-pasted JSONL traps (~24 inline occurrences), and no trap at all (7 pre-* dispatchers). Host project users who encounter a hook crash in a pre-* dispatcher get blocked with no diagnostic information. The logging trap is duplicated across hooks with only the hook name changed. New hook authors have no clear template.

## Success Criteria
- A shared _dso_hook_err_handler function exists in hooks/lib/hook-error-handler.sh that all fail-open hooks use
- All 7 pre-* dispatchers (pre-bash, pre-all, pre-edit, pre-write, pre-enterplanmode, pre-exitplanmode, pre-taskoutput) and all 5 post-* dispatchers have fail-open ERR traps using the shared handler
- All ~24 existing copy-pasted logging traps migrated to shared handler (targets: hooks/lib/pre-bash-functions.sh, pre-edit-write-functions.sh, pre-all-functions.sh, session-misc-functions.sh, review-gate-bypass-sentinel.sh; top-level: session-safety-check.sh, taskoutput-block-guard.sh, plan-review-gate.sh, track-cascade-failures.sh, title-length-validator.sh, track-tool-errors.sh, review-stop-check.sh, check-validation-failures.sh)
- Error logs written to ~/.claude/logs/dso-hook-errors.jsonl with enriched fields: timestamp, hook name, line number, repo root, plugin version, bash version, OS. Legacy path deprecated; all readers and test fixtures updated to new path
- Enforcement hooks explicitly excluded with machine-enforced boundary (header convention + pre-commit check)
- All existing tests pass
- At least one new test verifies ERR trigger produces correctly-structured JSONL entry with all enriched fields

## Scenario Analysis
- Shared handler source failure drops ERR traps (high): guard with || true and inline fallback
- ERR trap fires on intentional enforcement returns (medium): shared handler must not interfere with trap - ERR; return 2 pattern
- JSONL log grows unbounded (medium): handler should include size guard
- Dual competing log files during migration (high): readers must be updated atomically with writers
- Partial migration inconsistency (medium): migrate all ~24 sites in one epic
- Enforcement boundary is ambiguous (high): add convention + check

## Approach
Create _dso_hook_err_handler() in hooks/lib/hook-error-handler.sh. Handler disables ERR trap inside body, uses only builtins, caches plugin version on first source, falls back gracefully for git/path failures. Includes log size guard. Migrates all ~24 trap sites and all readers atomically. Adds enforcement hook boundary convention enforced by pre-commit check.

## Dependencies
None. Note: epic 0df8-1c8c (code quality) should complete first if it touches the same hook files.

comments.body: Adversarial review: 8 findings, 7 accepted, 1 rejected. Full exchange: null (artifact persistence not available in non-interactive mode). Key findings applied: S1 scope expanded (test-session-misc-no-jq.sh), legacy path dual-read during migration, S5 reordered to depend on S1 only (not S2/S3/S4), annotation-driven boundary check, source guard pattern for S2, return-semantics note for S4.
